pi_items.source: Code analysis: export_result.sh:5 defines SOURCE_FILE with identical path; setup_task.sh:60 removes this file; verifier.py:115 copies this file for inspection
Code analysis: export_result.sh:6 defines PDF_FILE with identical path; setup_task.sh:61 removes this file; export_result.sh:27 checks for PDF existence

Code analysis: (1) All 7 components appear in setup_task.sh specification document embedded in heredoc (lines 8-53); (2) verifier.py:129 hardcodes identical components list in lowercase; (3) Web search confirms SF-1 is standard HVAC supply fan notation; CC-1, HC-1, PF-1, FF-1, OAD-1, RAD-1 are project-specific numbered tags consistent with HVAC building controls nomenclature (https://www.shareddocs.com/hvac/docs/1000/Public/03/11-808-423-01.pdf)

Code analysis: (1) All 4 sensors appear in setup_task.sh specification (lines 38-41); (2) verifier.py:140 hardcodes identical sensors list in lowercase; (3) Web search confirms SAT=Supply Air Temperature (https://www.gmccx.com/post/supply-air-temperature-trouble), MAT=Mixed Air Temperature, RAT=Return Air Temperature (https://support.trane.com/hc/en-us/articles/4831030695949), DSP=Duct Static Pressure (https://manuals.plus/daikin/im-1311-duct-static-pressure-sensor-manual)

Specification analysis: AHU schematic requires minimum 8 physical flow connections (edges): OAD-1→Mixing, RAD-1→Mixing, Mixing→PF-1, PF-1→FF-1, FF-1→HC-1, HC-1→CC-1, CC-1→SF-1, SF-1→Discharge. This represents complete left-to-right airflow path as described in task description (line 6: 'Mixing -> Filter -> Heating -> Cooling -> Fan'). However, metadata value is NOT used by verifier.py; verifier performs content checks (components, sensors, dashed lines) but not graph edge validation.

privileged_info_summary: The task creates a hospital HVAC AHU-1 schematic diagram with 7 required components (supply fan SF-1, cooling coil CC-1, heating coil HC-1, pre-filter PF-1, final filter FF-1, outdoor air damper OAD-1, return air damper RAD-1) and 4 required sensors (SAT=Supply Air Temperature, MAT=Mixed Air Temperature, RAT=Return Air Temperature, DSP=Duct Static Pressure). The schematic uses MERV-8 and MERV-14 filters appropriate for hospital applications. Files are saved to /home/ga/Diagrams/AHU-1_Schematic.drawio and /home/ga/Diagrams/AHU-1_Schematic.pdf. The specification document created in setup_task.sh accurately describes a single-duct VAV system with economizer control, and all component/sensor tags match standard HVAC nomenclature and industry abbreviations verified through HVAC technical documentation.
